memory_definition.description: Memory initialization means future runtime code has established documented memory structures, selected controlled memory regions, documented ownership, and emitted governed evidence from runtime code.

evidence_requirements: KOZO_MEMORY_INIT_OK marker captured from runtime code
proof_boundary: evidence must come from runtime code, not scripts
evidence must be captured through QEMU serial smoke or a successor governed runtime evidence path
evidence must preserve the existing stack evidence marker sequence until a successor evidence path is accepted
evidence must not replace the halt loop until runtime progression evidence permits it
assumptions_enabled: controlled memory access after the proven memory marker
eligibility for runtime progression entry after memory and progression path evidence
safe memory-dependent preparation for later runtime initialization evidence
